title: 3) Balancing with muscle release
text:
The target of this method is not the dermis, but muscle. The purpose is different. This
technique is very popular in Korea and it started from the treatment of facial asymmetry
from Bell's palsy or TMJ disorders.
The  main  purpose  is  to  adjust  muscle  tensegrity  by  releasing  muscles/fasciae  with
needles. Needles on a tightened muscle can slacken tightness and remove trigger points.
Also, needles on a weakened muscle can help strengthen the muscle, usually with E-stim
(electrical muscle stimulation). For instance, in case of Bell's palsy, we apply acupuncture
stimulation on the paralyzed side with E-stim, and use acupuncture on another side (pulled
side) to remove trigger points and release the muscles. It can be applied for a cosmetic
purpose.
For symmetry
First, symmetry is an important part of beauty, but none of us are perfectly symmetric. We
all have different left and right balance, and this difference is from an asymmetry of muscle
tensegrity. The masseter muscles on right side and left side may be not equal in size. When
we laugh, one side of the lip tends to be elevated more. One eye may be saggier than the
other.
All these conditions are affected by muscle tension. With muscle release acupuncture, we
can  help  them  to  balance.  To  balance  the  face,  we  start  from  neck,  that  is  why  the
sternocleidomastoid (SCM) and the trapezius are the must-check muscles for treating balance
of face.
For dynamic wrinkles
Second, unlike static wrinkles, dynamic wrinkles are closely related to muscles. Try to
raise your eyebrow as much as possible, you would make forehead lines and probably one
side has deeper wrinkles and a larger eye. Wrinkle depth is determined by muscle movement
when the movement is consistent and habitual.
If you are a heavy smoker, you will have a bigger chance of developing smoker lines, not
because of the cigarette, but because of the constant contraction of the orbicularis oris, which
makes dynamic wrinkles around lips. Some people smirk without intending to, because their
levator labii superioris is usually tightened. In this case, if you can release those muscles,
they can have a happier face. Not only the tightened muscles are a problem. Loosened
muscles can be a problem too. A major reason of saggy skin is the loss of fat.
~ !  ~
43
Facial  Cosmetic Acupuncture
However, when the muscle (which is holding the skin against gravity) gets loose, the skin
can become more saggy or droopy. As people get older, eyelids get saggy for this reason. A
weakened zygomaticus plays a role in making marionette lines.  In 2013, Younghee Yun, et
al.  reported that muscle/fascia cosmetic acupuncture can help with saggy skin of the face.
12
This was a controlled study with objective measures. They compared Moire topography
instead of a photo or subjective score.
Younghee Yun, et al. Effect of facial cosmetic acupuncture on facial elasticity: an open-label, single-arm pilot study.
12
Evidence Based Complement Alternative Med, 2013:424313.
~ !  ~

Needling by balancing with muscle release
Needling on the related muscles
• Depressor labii inferioris
• Depressor Anguli Oris
Facial  Cosmetic Acupuncture
